title: rubus × scrupeus
intro: Rubus × scrupeus, also known as the hybrid bramble or hybrid blackberry, is a complex hybrid species of the Rubus genus, resulting from the cross between the European bramble (Rubus fruticosus) and the blackberry (Rubus argutus). The leaves and stems of the plant contain tannins, which possess medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus × scrupeus include its potential to help reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus × scrupeus are typically made from the leaves, stems, and roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
